creator: gslin@gslin.org
text:
On Tue, Mar 09, 2010 at 10:36:15AM +0100, Martin Matuska wrote:
> Are you sure all these ports compile and run afterwards with X264
> enabled? :-)
> 
> multimedia/arista
> multimedia/avidemux2
> multimedia/ffmpeg
> multimedia/ffmpeg-devel
> multimedia/gstreamer-plugins-x264
> multimedia/handbrake
> multimedia/libquicktime
> multimedia/lives
> multimedia/mencoder
> multimedia/mpeg4ip
> multimedia/mplayer
> multimedia/ogmrip
> multimedia/transcode
> multimedia/vlc
> multimedia/xfce4-parole
> net/opal3
> 
> To make this update reality, ALL PORTS ABOVE have to work with it.
> 
> I will update ffmpeg soon to 0.5.1 that supports recent x264 versions, I
> am going to update ffmpeg-devel as well.
> Vlc should be no problem (as x264 is their own project).
> libquicktime is my port as well.
> The other ports are a question, though. The worst case is to have two
> x264's in the ports tree.
> 

I've tried serveral ports you mentioned above, and almost all of them
cannot compile with latest x264 (after change dependency from "x264.65"
to "x264").

Two ports might be much easier way to maintain these ports.

BTW, please close ports/140865, which update x264 to 0.0.20091124.

Thanks,

-- 
* Gea-Suan Lin  (public key: Using https://keyserver.pgp.com/ to search)
* If you cannot convince them, confuse them.           -- Harry S Truman
